inline_comments.message:
@Toine & all:
I have problem in testing the ancestor case.
The exitAncestorAxis() in CpsPathBuilder returns categories[@code=1] instead of categories[@code='1'].
I have tried to use enterAncestorAxis but i guess its not the place for fix. (not working anyway)
The exitAncestorAxis() gets the AncestorAxisContext from the ancestorPath() in CpsPathParser (generated code).

Any pointers would be helpful.
variable is "wasWrappedInDoubleQuote" but only checking if it starts with one but not end with one.
Just 2022 if it is a new file created this year

The intention is to check whether the input is wrapped inside double quotes. So in the exitLeafCondition() if it starts with double quotes then we assume that the string literal is wrapped inside the double quotes so we need to escape it to save the value with double quotes in the DB.
agreed, the parse will have checked (have passed) so there is no need to check last token here
suggestion: maybe we should add 'Builder' to the name...

i wonder should this not be in an else block below ie. when NOT 'processingAncestorAxis'
(not 100% suer though)
no need for this check here (look at the method name :-))
for future proofing you shoudl set processinAncestorAxis to false here...
suggestion: maybe we should add 'Builder' to the name...
ok I see you need to update this one always
this can be moved in enterMultipleLeafConsition once rule refactor is applied
this can be moved in exitMultipleLeafConsition once rule refactor is applied

I think you missed my previous comment. Remove the comment if it is no longer relevant :)
No need for where block if using only a single row. Just inline the values instead,

- etc.

dont need this in this case

is assume it is to validate it and make it thrown an exception, if so extract out into a validateXPAth method or alternatively (better I think now) implement a CpsPathUtil.isValidateXpath() method that either throws an exception is returns a boolean...

Refactoring results in some of the test cases fails:

Condition not satisfied:

result.hasLeafConditions() == false
|      |                   |
|      true                false
<org.onap.cps.cpspath.parser.CpsPathQuery@3961a41a xpathPrefix=/descendant normalizedXpath=//descendant/categories[@key1='1' and @key2='2'] cpsPathPrefixType=DESCENDANT descendantName=descendant leavesData=[key1:1, key2:2] ancestorSchemaNodeIdentifier=categories[@key1='1' and @key2='2'] textFunctionConditionLeafName=null textFunctionConditionValue=null>

Further debugging needed
change of grammar causes some test to fail. Further debugging needed.
The normalised path will be used at line 402. updated in next patch
I haven't checked that yet. I need more information on how to test it in liquidbase.
Sorry I mean should it be ...getText().startsWith("\"").endsWith("\"") as the boolean name is wasWrappedInDoubleQuote
A compound key would be "categories[@code='1' and @code2='2']"

I'm not sure this is needed because if the first string starts with double quotes our ANTLR grammar force it should end with double quotes otherwise it will throw error and it will never reach this line. The wasWrappedInDoubleQuote is for our understand that the string literal was wrapped in double quotes like this,
'/parent/child[@code="\'1\'"]' 

If this variable name is confusing or misleading we can change it to different one.
This looks aligned in my IDE. :(
ok, it is not so improtant lets not get distracted by it. I'll explore it later when your fix is done
depending on further us of this exception (see upcoming comments) teh message might be hardcode in teh exception class ie. just need a constructor with the details....
should we use the new CpsPathException here too ?!
PathParsingException (when use in context of query service there might be other reasons for it being 'invalid' so lets be more specific in the name)
see my oterh commeten maybe we can use teh new CpsPathException in both cases...

I want to get back to this once we are using CpsPath exception for all scenarios.
(not sure if the message need to be variable (adn verified here) but it is more interesting to check that the details contain the original path or specificc abour error...)
Not sure, because this was there before. I think having CpsPathException makes sense but it is created in cps-service and referenced in cps-rest module.
Inly checking if it starts with " but not ending with " ... wasWrappedInDoubleQuotes should check both I would assume
Same as line 149... so if processing ancestor axis we add the same information?
No need for constant if only used once. Just inline instead.
Is this duplicating scenarios on lines 70 & 71? Looks like this test is redundant?

The intention is to check whether the input is wrapped inside double quotes. So in the exitLeafCondition() if it starts with double quotes then we assume that the string literal is wrapped inside the double quotes so we need to escape it to save the value with double quotes in the DB.
It checks the getNormalizedXpath(). It is misleading because of the cps path in the scenario. Updated with xpath in next patch
